title: AI Operations Engineer
company_focus:
SaaS-based School Information Systems for K-12 independent
schools
description:
Company Description

Veracross provides SaaS-based School Information Systems
(SIS) designed to meet the specific needs of independent
K-12 schools worldwide. Our one-record solution combines the
power of a fully integrated single-record database,
personalized communication tools, and an elegant
architecture that is unique in our industry.

We are a growing, values-led community of 350 plus employees
in the US, UK and Australia who share a vision to unify
school communities, improve the quality of education, and
enhance learning. And we’re succeeding!

Veracross is five product brands in one global tech company

- Veracross SIS is a one-person, one-record school management
  platform
- Magnus Health provides cloud-based Student Health Record
  (SHR) solutions
- Digistorm connects with their communities through Digistorm
  Websites, Digistorm Funnel, and Digistorm Apps.
- Epraise incentivizes student well-being and connects
  teachers, students, and families.
- Firefly provides an online learning space for students and
  teachers

About the Role

We’re forming a new AI & Data Operations team and hiring our
first AI Platform Engineer to bring safe, practical AI
operations into our EdTech products. You’ll work end‑to‑end
across the AI stack – from infrastructure to shipping
features.

This is a hands‑on role. You’ll partner closely with
engineering leadership and product teams to ship AI features
that help schools, while ensuring reliability, safety, and
cost control.

What You’ll Do

Build AI‑Powered Product Features

- Design and ship agentic AI products to optimize and automate
  school operations

- Build systems using school‑specific data and institutional
  knowledge; develop prompt strategies and reusable
  libraries

- Integrate AI capabilities into existing Ruby on Rails
  applications via APIs and SDKs

Operate, Optimize & Scale LLM Systems

- Curate and maintain our AWS AI infrastructure (Bedrock,
  GuardRails, etc.)

- Evaluate and select models, benchmark quality, latency, and
  cost on school operations tasks

- Optimize prompts, caching, batching, and workflows to
  improve performance and reduce spend

- Monitor token usage and implement budgeting and cost
  controls; run A/B tests across approaches

Safety, Guardrails & Compliance

- Design guardrails to prevent hallucinations, bias, and
  inappropriate outputs

- Detect and redact PII in AI inputs/outputs; establish safety
  protocols for K–12

- Build feedback loops for human review and continuous
  improvement

Observability & Evaluation

- Instrument AI systems with logging, metrics, and tracing;
  build dashboards for quality, latency, cost, and safety

- Create evaluation datasets and automated testing pipelines,
  incorporate user feedback into ongoing improvements

Research & Enablement

- Stay current with the AI/ML ecosystem; prototype new models,
  techniques, and tools

- Evaluate and select AI hosting solutions (AWS AgentCore,
  Cloudflare Agents, etc.)

What You’ll Build (First 12 Months)

- AI‑powered features with measurable impact on school
  operations

- Core AI operations infrastructure: evaluation, monitoring,
  and guardrails

- Scalable best practices for responsible AI across our
  products

Requirements

- 5+ years of software engineering experience, including
  hands‑on LLM work

- Experience building and operating production AI applications

- Strong understanding of prompt engineering LLM best
  practices

- Comfortable working with APIs in a polyglot environment

Strongly Preferred

- Applied AI skills

- Ruby on Rails experience (or willingness to learn quickly)
  or similar frameworks

- Experience in regulated or safety‑sensitive domains

Personal Qualities

- Pragmatic, production‑minded, and security focused

- Curious and fast‑learning; clear communicator with technical
  and non‑technical audiences

- Mission‑driven about improving school operations

Why This Role Is Unique

- One of the first roles shaping AI solutions across our
  entire product portfolio

- Direct impact on the quality of life of our users

- Modern AI solving real problems—not research for research’s
  sake

- Work on a roadmap with strong executive support on a small
  team with big influence

Our Tech Environment

Ruby on Rails applications • SQL Server, Postgres & MySQL •
AWS cloud infrastructure

You’ll have meaningful input into tool selection, workflows,
and long‑term AI strategy.

Benefits

- Our team is fully remote- everyone works from home
- 25 days holiday, plus public holidays
- 4% matched pension contributions
- Payroll Giving
- Private medical
- Discounted Gym Membership
- Cycle Scheme
- Enhanced Sick pay
- Enhanced Maternity, Paternity, Adoption and shared Paternity
  Leave pay.
- Employee Assistance Program

use_cases: Automating school operations with agentic AI products
Safe AI operations for regulated educational environments
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is an ai-first role focused on building, deploying, and
operating AI/LLM systems end-to-end. The engineer will ship
agentic AI products, build prompt strategies, maintain AWS
AI infrastructure (Bedrock), implement guardrails, and
optimize LLM performance—all direct hands-on work with AI
systems.
